target.title: fix(memory-core): index memory path in FTS text for filename queries (fixes #94102)
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #94102.
- `memory_search` FTS rows indexed only chunk body text, so queries for date-like filename stems (e.g. `2026-06-17-1649`) returned unrelated files with `textScore: 0` even when the target file was indexed and content search worked.
- Include the memory file path in the FTS `text` column at index time (`${entry.path}\n${chunk.text}`) so filename/date tokens participate in hybrid keyword ranking.
- Vector embeddings and chunk body text are unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:
Exact filename/date-token queries fail to rank the matching memory file first despite a clean index and working content search.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux (WSL2), isolated FTS-only workspace (`memorySearch.provider: none`), built from this branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
PROOF_ROOT=$(mktemp -d)
mkdir -p "$PROOF_ROOT/workspace/memory" "$PROOF_ROOT/state"
# openclaw.json points workspace at $PROOF_ROOT/workspace, provider none, hybrid enabled
printf '%s\n' 'Token has been expired or revoked Google OAuth Testing mode' > "$PROOF_ROOT/workspace/memory/2026-06-17-1649.md"
printf '%s\n' 'all important calendars PM Recurring Tasks Todoist highest-rigor' > "$PROOF_ROOT/workspace/memory/2026-06-17-1701.md"
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$PROOF_ROOT/state" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$PROOF_ROOT/openclaw.json" pnpm openclaw memory index --force
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$PROOF_ROOT/state" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$PROOF_ROOT/openclaw.json" pnpm openclaw memory search "2026-06-17-1649" --json
```

Evidence after fix:
Live `openclaw memory search` JSON after FTS reindex on the patched branch (snippet shows path prefix in indexed FTS text):

```json
{
  "results": [
    {
      "path": "memory/2026-06-17-1649.md",
      "score": 1,
      "textScore": 0.000003947886006030355,
      "snippet": "memory/2026-06-17-1649.md\nToken has been expired or revoked Google OAuth Testing mode\n",
      "source": "memory"
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix:
Top keyword result path is `memory/2026-06-17-1649.md` with `textScore > 0`; nearby file `memory/2026-06-17-1701.md` is not returned for the filename stem query.

What was not tested:
Live Gemini-embedded workspace index with provider credentials (requires reindex against an existing semantic index).

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts -t "filename token ranking"`
- `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=... OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=... pnpm openclaw memory index --force`
- `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=... OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=... pnpm openclaw memory search "2026-06-17-1649" --json`

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
Highest-risk area:
Hybrid memory search keyword ranking for existing indexes
Mitigation:
Change applies on reindex/write paths only; no config surface. Existing indexes need reindex to pick up path tokens in FTS text.

## Current review state

Next action:
Await CI + ClawSweeper review after proof gate refresh
Waiting on:
Maintainer review
Bot comments addressed:
Updated Real behavior proof with live `openclaw memory search` terminal output (replaces mock-only vitest-only proof)

AI-assisted: yes
target.author: pick-cat
